sglang.title: [Feat] Support FlashMLA backend with MTP and FP8 KV cache
sglang.author: quinnrong94
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

This PR improves flashmla backend by accelerating decode stage with mtp. The implementation utilizes the feature that flashmla can handle seq_len_q > 1. To use flashmla with mtp, an example server args can be --attention-backend flashmla --speculative-algorithm NEXTN --speculative-num-st

sglang.author: Alcanderian
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Modifications

Checklist

 Format your code according to the Code Formatting with Pre-Commit.
 Add unit tests as outlined in the Running Unit Tests.
 Update documentation / docstrings / example tutorials as needed, according to Writing Documentation.
 Provide throughput / latency benchma

sglang.title: Add fp8 gemm kernel for CPU in sgl-kernel and add gemm UT
sglang.author: chunyuan-w
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

This PR is a follow-up on #2807 and #5150 to add fp8 gemm kernel for CPU. The bf16 and int8 gemm kernel is already added in #5150.
This PR also adds UTs for bf16, int8 and fp8 gemm kernels for CPU.
Modifications
The main change is the C++ kernels for fp8 gemm on CPU: sgl-kernel/csrc/cpu/

vllm.title: [Kernel] [V1] Fix performance regression for triton unified attention
vllm.author: tdoublep
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
We have observed a pretty severe (~40%) performance regression for the triton_unified_attention kernel when moving from triton 3.2 to triton 3.3.
After a lot of investigation, I was able to figure out that it came from this commit to Triton. This change reworked the way that Triton determines what k

FIX #18189

vllm.title: [Bugfix]Change the exception thrown by call_hf_processor from RuntimeError to ValueError
vllm.author: Abatom
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
In our multimodal scenario, when the input image size does not match the model's requirements, the call_hf_processor function throws a RuntimeError.
In the async_llm_engine.py, the engine_step function only catches ValueError, which leads to that the client hangs and cannot exit. If the call_hf_proc

vllm.title: [Build] Allow shipping PTX on a per-file basis
vllm.author: LucasWilkinson
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
323.20 MB -> 324.36 MB
To help with the growing wheel size due to Blackwell allow for shipping PTX for heavy kernels that don't take advantage of new hardware features. Theres enough different gencodes now for certain kernels it makes sense to ship a single PTX implementation instead of multiple SAS

vllm.title: [Bugfix][ROCm] Use `chunked_prefill_paged_decode` as fallback for V1 attention on ROCm
vllm.author: kliuae
vllm.bodyExcerpt: On ROCm, vLLM’s V1 engine uses the unified attention kernel as its sole attention backend. However, at the moment this kernel fails when running models where the number of query heads over the number of key-value heads is not a power of two. This makes models like Llama-4-Scout, whose num_queries_pe

vllm.bodyExcerpt: vLLM v1 supports Medusa. This is a basic version, and I will support cudaGraph in the future

vllm.title: Allow users to pass arbitrary JSON keys from CLI
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Closes #17640
Supersede #17842
Allows users to pass individual JSON keys via FlexibleArgumentParser in the format --json-arg.key1.key2 value
i.e. --hf-overrides.key1 val1 --hf-overrides.key2.key3 val2 will result in the following args:
INFO 05-15 17:09:16 [cli_args.py:297] non-default args: {'enforc

vllm.title: Throw better error for when running into k8s service discovery issue
vllm.author: wseaton
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Add a better error message if a user is likely running into the k8s service discovery issue: https://docs.vllm.ai/en/stable/serving/env_vars.html#environment-variables
I lost 20 minutes on this due to the default int() -> ValueError message , I think a better one would help.

vllm.title: [Feature] Support Pipeline Parallism in torchrun SPMD offline inference for V1
vllm.author: luccafong
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR add support for PP on torchrun offline inference, note this now does not support overlapping mircobatches so not the most efficient way to ublock PP use cases, will improve as follow ups.

We can't run the script with --help to see the help message without connecting to a running vLLM server. This change allows us to do that.
We also improve the error message when the script cannot get a list of models
from vLLM.
